concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: titles and complete available abstracts of all 637 assigned records, with boundary checks against explicit geographic-spread papers
concept_decisions.rationale: The manifested records are overwhelmingly phylodynamic, coalescent, genealogy-based, or molecular-epidemiologic studies that infer epidemic history, transmission dynamics, or effective population processes from sequence data. The provisional canonical name is a fallback bucket; renaming is required to match the actual estimand.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: titles and complete available abstracts of all 636 assigned records plus three explicitly geographic genomic-diffusion records reassigned in
concept_decisions.rationale: This cluster is coherently defined by sequence-based inference on geographic origin, migration, dispersal, and spatial diffusion. The manifested papers consistently target where lineages move, spread, or originate rather than only reconstructing transmission history.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: titles and complete available abstracts of all 10 assigned records
concept_decisions.rationale: All assigned papers are Hawkes or closely related latent/marked self-exciting point-process formulations for infectious spread, sharing a common event-triggering identification structure.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: no manifested records were assigned; exact batch coverage is zero
concept_decisions.rationale: The label is already a precise design concept, and the bounded evidence contains no assigned records that justify merging, splitting, or renaming it.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: titles and complete available abstracts of all 18 assigned records plus the three spatial_regression_geostatistics records needed to resolve the boundary
concept_decisions.rationale: A coherent mechanistic subset uses diffusion operators, travelling waves, PDE/compartmental reaction-diffusion structure, or diffusion-based control analysis. The remaining assigned papers are empirical geographic spread/risk-surface studies or an explicitly phylogeographic genomic-diffusion study, so the provisional concept requires a true split.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: titles and complete available abstracts of all 3 assigned records
concept_decisions.rationale: The assigned records do not support a standalone spatial-regression/geostatistics concept in this batch. One record is an empirical kriging-based spread analysis and belongs with empirical spatial diffusion, while two records are coalescent/genealogy-based phylodynamic inference papers and belong with phylodynamic transmission-history inference.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: titles and complete available abstracts of all 39 assigned records
concept_decisions.rationale: Across surveillance, intervention, vaccination, microbiome, and epidemic-model applications, the common design task is structural-break or breakpoint detection in time series, regression, or transmission dynamics. The manifested set stays methodologically coherent at this level.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: titles and complete available abstracts of all 515 assigned records
concept_decisions.rationale: The manifested records span operational early-warning systems, leading indicators, alerting frameworks, surveillance signals, and warning scores. Renaming broadens the canonical name to match the actual observed design space without relying on a fallback label.

reassignments.rationale: The paper explicitly reconstructs phylo-geographic relationships and common geographic origin from sequence data, matching genomic spatial-diffusion inference rather than generic phylodynamic history.
The paper estimates relative migration rates and geographic spread with BEAST, making geographic diffusion the core estimand.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: This is an empirical regression-plus-GIS risk-mapping study of influenza climate factors and spatial risk zones, not a mechanistic reaction-diffusion model.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: This paper empirically reconstructs pandemic spread using mortality, mobility, demographic, and environmental data rather than diffusion operators in a mechanistic epidemic system.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: The study identifies observed arboviral diffusion patterns using kernel maps, nearest-neighbour ratios, and spatial autocorrelation, which is empirical spread analysis rather than reaction-diffusion modeling.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: This is an empirical comparison of Zika, dengue, and chikungunya diffusion patterns using kernel maps, correlograms, LISA, and nearest-neighbour modeling.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: The paper uses space-time scan statistics to identify observed COVID-19 clusters, which is empirical spatial spread analysis rather than reaction-diffusion dynamics.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: This paper studies changing empirical diffusion drivers across Chinese cities using spatial association analysis, mobility, and covariates, not mechanistic diffusion equations.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: The study estimates regional heterogeneity, seasonality, and observed spatial diffusion patterns from surveillance time series rather than using reaction-diffusion structure.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: This is an empirical mobility-regression analysis of COVID-19 spatial diffusion across South Korean counties, not a mechanistic diffusion model.
The paper uses a Bayesian phylodynamic framework on viral sequences to infer spatial diffusion patterns and migration pathways, which is phylogeographic genomic diffusion.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
reassignments.rationale: This record uses kriging and sentinel surveillance data to describe observed norovirus spatial spread, fitting empirical spatial diffusion/risk-surface analysis.
This is coalescent-based nonparametric phylodynamic inference on population size dynamics from genealogies, not geostatistical surface modeling.
This paper fits stochastic epidemic models to gene genealogies using linear noise approximation, which is phylodynamic transmission-history inference rather than geostatistics.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Empirical spatial diffusion and risk-surface analyses
new_concepts.rationale: Empirical, non-sequence-based analyses that reconstruct or map observed geographic spread using GIS interpolation, spatial autocorrelation, scan statistics, mobility regressions, kernel methods, or related risk-surface techniques.

concerns: border_discontinuity_designs has zero manifested records in this batch, so its retention is structurally precise but not abstract-supported within the bounded evidence.
